channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Study Examines How Pediatric Flu Vaccination Rates Affect US Healthcare Utilization
item.description: Reaching the Healthy People 2030 target of 70% childhood flu vaccination could meaningfully reduce influenza-related outpatient visits and hospitalizations across the US.

item.title: Research Links Adequate Sleep to Reduced Dementia Risk in Focal Epilepsy Patients
item.description: A study finds that optimal sleep duration is associated with improved executive function, with a notably stronger effect observed in patients diagnosed with focal epilepsy.

item.title: WashU Researchers Identify Distinct Protein Signatures Across Four Brain Diseases
item.description: Scientists at WashU Medicine have analyzed proteins in blood and spinal fluid to uncover molecular differences between Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and other forms of dementia.

item.title: Therapy Targeting Lost Pleasure Outperforms Standard Treatment for Depression
item.description: A new study finds that addressing anhedonia — reduced ability to feel pleasure — more effectively relieves depression and anxiety than conventional therapeutic approaches.

item.title: Optical Imaging Method Enables Automated Analysis of Brain White Matter
item.description: Researchers have developed a label-free optical imaging technique that allows automated measurement of white matter microstructure, supporting study of brain connectivity pathways.
